rank_note: Rank intentionally null. site/src/data/indexes/us-states.json contains only 21 of 51 jurisdictions; ranks 9-38 were lost in the original legacy-HTML extraction and the survivors were renumbered contiguously, so the displayed rank column is not a national rank. New Jersey must not be assigned a rank until the index is rebuilt with all 51 jurisdictions.
calibration_note: Not calibrated to the published us-states rank order (known corrupt). Calibrated to methodology anchors and to the eleven states assessed at full depth on 2026-07-19: Georgia 26.9, Iowa 30.6, Arizona 36.2, Ohio 38.1, Alaska 47.5, Colorado 52.5, Michigan 53.8, Delaware 53.8, Pennsylvania 54.4, New York 56.9, Illinois 60.0. New Jersey at 60.6 sits just above Illinois as the strongest state assessed to date, driven by first-in-nation environmental justice permitting upheld on appeal, Cover All Kids, statewide ARRIVE Together, and binding fourth-round housing obligations, and held back by the worst Black-white youth incarceration disparity in the United States, the June 2024 OPRA rollback, and a shelter system that cannot meet demand. Composite verified with computeCompositeFromDimensions in site/scripts/lib/scoring.mjs: base composite 60.625, stdDev 0.393 (consistencyMult 1.0), 7 of 8 dimensions below 4.0 so weaknessFactor 0 and integrationPremium 0, final composite 60.6, band Established. BAND FRAGILITY: 60.6 is 0.6 above the 60.0 Functional/Established boundary. Verified sensitivity cases run through the same function: lowering S3 from 4 to 3 yields 60.0 Functional; lowering E2 from 4 to 3 yields 60.0 Functional; raising A5 from 4 to 5 yields 61.2 Established; raising B3 from 2 to 3 yields 61.2 Established. The Established band label should be treated as provisional.
attribution_note: Scores New Jersey state conduct only. Federal-adjacent areas are scored only where the state exercised genuine discretion. The 2021 Edna Mahan consent decree and the veterans homes consent decree were federally initiated, so New Jersey is scored for its compliance and remediation conduct rather than for the underlying federal finding, except that Murphy's 2021 closure order preceded any court compulsion and is fully scored. Delaney Hall is a federally contracted immigration detention facility; New Jersey is scored only for its own legislative response, specifically the 2026 laws barring local ICE detention contracts and the two immigrant protection bills Murphy left unsigned on 2026-01-20. Federal Medicaid and housing funding cuts referenced in 2025-2026 reporting are not scored against New Jersey; the state's own Affordable Housing Trust Fund diversions are scored. The Charlie and Nadine H. child welfare exit is scored because New Jersey negotiated and met the exit plan and legislated a successor oversight body.
key_evidence: Murphy signed S2930 on 2024-06-05, effective 2024-09-03, deleting the statutory clause construing the Open Public Records Act in favor of public access, narrowing attorney-fee recovery to unreasonable denial, bad faith, or knowing and willful violation, and extending agency deadlines to 14 and 21 business days - a material transparency regression by a state with a strong policy reputation.

The New Jersey Appellate Division unanimously upheld the Environmental Justice Law rules on 2026-01-05, affirming the first US state regime requiring permit denial on disproportionate impact in overburdened communities and expressly rejecting economic benefit as a tradeoff for pollution.

New Jersey has the worst Black-white youth incarceration disparity in the United States, with Black youth 18 times more likely to be incarcerated than white youth, while the state has litigated the Latino Action Network school segregation case for eight years; mediation failed in February 2025 and the state Supreme Court declined to fast-track the case in May 2026.

The Office of the State Comptroller reported on 2026-01-14 that about 60 Police and Firemen's Retirement System members with misconduct records were drawing benefits and 21 had never received a required honorable-service review; a November 2025 bill to strip the Comptroller's corruption-investigation powers was withdrawn after public objection.

Murphy ordered Edna Mahan Correctional Facility closed in 2021 after more than a dozen officers were arrested for assaulting incarcerated women; the $310 million replacement broke ground 2025-10-15, opens 2027 and completes in 2029 - a six-to-eight year gap between order and delivery.
The 2025 statewide point-in-time count found 13,748 people homeless, the highest since 2014, with unsheltered homelessness up 103% since 2022 and the shelter system running above 90% capacity daily.

Governor Sherrill took office 2026-01-20 and declared a state of emergency on utility costs on day one, ordering the Board of Public Utilities to issue bill credits by 2026-07-01; environmental justice rules, ARRIVE Together, Cover All Kids and the fourth-round housing obligations all survived the Murphy-to-Sherrill transition intact.

evidence.quote: The new law guarantees a requestor attorney's fees only if the public agency unreasonably denied access, acted in bad faith, or knowingly and willfully violated OPRA
evidence.claim: state narrowed its own public records law, a documented transparency regression

evidence.quote: The court affirmed that claims of economic benefits cannot be used to allow new sources of pollution in overburdened communities
evidence.claim: first-in-nation structural harm-prevention regime upheld on independent judicial review

evidence.quote: New Jersey has the worst racial disparity in America, with a Black youth being 18 times more likely to be incarcerated than a white youth
evidence.claim: nation-worst outcome disparity persists despite the state measuring it openly
evidence.source: New Jersey Institute for Social Justice

evidence.quote: OSC identified almost 60 members of the Police and Firemen's Retirement System who had a record of misconduct and were receiving pension benefits
evidence.claim: active, independent state oversight producing unflattering findings the state then acted on
evidence.source: NJ Office of the State Comptroller

evidence.quote: a decaying 112-year-old facility in Clinton that Gov. Phil Murphy in 2021 ordered closed after more than a dozen correctional officers were arrested for brutally assaulting incarcerated women
evidence.claim: strong-law, slow-delivery pattern: closure ordered 2021, replacement completes 2029

evidence.quote: an overburdened shelter system that, in recent months, has consistently operated at more than 90% capacity daily and cannot accommodate the increasing number of households
evidence.claim: refusal without alternative; the largest single drag on the composite
evidence.source: Monarch Housing Associates NJ point-in-time report

evidence.quote: Among the pregnancy-related deaths in 2019-2023, 27.6% were due at least in part to discrimination, interpersonal racism, and structural racism
evidence.claim: publicly unflattering self-disclosure by the state's own review process

evidence.quote: Governor Sherrill Takes Bold Action with Executive Orders Declaring State of Emergency on Utility Costs
evidence.claim: day-one responsiveness in the most heavily weighted window; transition did not degrade care infrastructure
evidence.source: Office of the Governor of New Jersey
